code_change.summary: Added TORCHTITAN_DSV4_SPARSE_MLA_SHAREDKV_TILELANG_HEAD_BLOCK for the no-HSK direct-source D-blocked TileLang dKV reducer and folded the new head tile into the existing 3D TileLang launch axis.

results.read: Baseline direct-source D-blocked schedule compiles and matches the BF16 dKV reference, but is already much slower than the S128 PyTorch explicit smoke.
Head splitting reduces shared-memory pressure enough to compile at 128 threads, but the improvement is only about 4.2% and remains far too slow at S128.

results.failure: TileLang ROCm GEMM layout inference divide-by-zero.
results.read: The 256-thread H32 layout is not legal in this TileLang/ROCm lowering tuple.

results.failure: TileLang ROCm GEMM layout inference divide-by-zero.
results.read: Going narrower than H32 is not currently a viable TileLang MFMA layout.

decision.reason: The only compiling split-head variant is still about 8x slower than the S128 explicit PyTorch smoke and only about 4% faster than full-H direct-source D-blocked. Running this schedule at S4096 would chase an already-failed topology.
next_kernel_direction: Do not continue with TileLang group-owned or D-blocked source recompute schedules.
Keep the materialized-input selected-limit reducer as diagnostic evidence only.

Move the no-HSK compact reducer to a lower-level HIP/CK/rocWMMA segmented producer, or wait for a stronger TileLang primitive that can keep query-major P/dP reuse while doing sparse grouped accumulation without large HSK slabs.
